title: Mouthwatering Smoky Mexican Pork Cochinita Pibil
description: A cultured and juicy Mexican dish featuring slow roasted pork marinated in citrus and spices, cooked in peanut oil with a smoky flavor, halal friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a blender or food processor, combine orange juice, lime juice, peanut oil, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, black pepper, garlic, and onion, and blend until smooth.
Place the pork shoulder in a large Dutch oven or oven safe pot, and pour the marinade over the top.
Cover the pot with a lid, and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the pork for 6 hours, or until it is tender and easily shreds with a fork.
Remove the pot from the oven, and let it cool slightly.
Shred the pork with two forks, and stir to coat with the juices.
Serve the cochinita pibil hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro, onion, and lime wedges.
